# Homepage Restyle (Round 2) Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Restyle the OpenTikZ landing page — reorder the "Why" sections, add an on-page Get-started install section, restyle the "Why TikZ" reasons as numbered cards, lighten the hero carousel arrows to flat chevrons, delete the roadmap teaser, and standardize section rules.

**Architecture:** The whole site is generated by one stdlib script, `tools/build_site.py`. The home page is assembled in `home_page()` from helper band functions (`why_tikz_band()`, `why_opentikz_band()`, `howto_carousel()`); all styling lives in the `STYLE_CSS` raw string in the same file. There is **no test framework** — verification is "run the generator, then grep the emitted HTML/CSS for expected presence/absence." Task 1 changes markup (Python helper functions that emit HTML); Task 2 changes `STYLE_CSS`.

**Tech Stack:** Python 3 stdlib (f-strings / raw strings), hand-written HTML + CSS. No dependencies, no test runner.

## Global Constraints

- Scope is **the landing page only**: `home_page()` and `STYLE_CSS` in `tools/build_site.py`, plus one one-line link fix in `skills_page()`. Do **not** touch `/browse/`, template/icon content, `catalog.json`, `README.md`, or `CLAUDE.md`.
- Colors only via existing CSS variables (`--otblue --otorange --otteal --paper --ink --muted --line --line-strong`) — never inline hex in new CSS.
- The new Get-started section's install commands MUST match the How-to-use page's recommended card verbatim: `/plugin marketplace add https://github.com/opentikz/opentikz` then `/plugin install opentikz@opentikz`. Use the existing `REPO_URL` constant (`= "https://github.com/opentikz/opentikz"`) so the two never drift.
- New section order (top→bottom): `hero → Get started → 4 ways to use → Why TikZ → Why not ask ChatGPT → cta-band`. The roadmap section is deleted.
- Every home section boundary = exactly one `border-top:1px solid var(--line)` on the lower section. `.showcase` (first) has none.
- After changes, `python3 tools/build_site.py` must succeed and `git status` content tree must be otherwise unchanged (no preview/catalog regeneration — no content changed).
- Verification = build + grep. The build writes to `site/`; grep `site/index.html`, `site/skills/index.html`, `site/assets/style.css`.

---

### Task 1: Markup — reorder, new Get-started band, restyle hooks, roadmap deletion

**Files:**
- Modify: `tools/build_site.py` — `hero_carousel()` (~L421-427), `why_tikz_band()` (~L431-442), `why_opentikz_band()` (~L445-474), `home_page()` (~L477-533), `skills_page()` (~L677-681). Add new `getstarted_band()` helper.

**Interfaces:**
- Produces: `getstarted_band() -> str` (no args; reads module-level `REPO_URL`). Called by `home_page()`.
- Produces HTML hooks consumed by Task 2's CSS: `<section class="getstarted" id="get-started">`, `<p class="gs-lede">`, `<div class="gs-cmds">`, `<p class="gs-note">`; `<span class="wts-num">`/`<span class="wts-desc">` inside each `.wts-item`; hero chevron glyphs `&lsaquo;`/`&rsaquo;` on `.car-nav` buttons inside `.hero-carousel`.

- [ ] **Step 1: Baseline — confirm the current strings exist (will change)**

Run:
```bash
cd /Users/xyz/Documents/Projects/opentikz && python3 tools/build_site.py >/dev/null && \
grep -c 'On the roadmap' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'why-ot-sub' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'href="skills/">Get started' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'index.html#roadmap' site/skills/index.html
```
Expected: `1`, `1`, `1`, `1` (each present in the current build — these are what we remove/repoint).

- [ ] **Step 2: Swap the hero carousel arrow glyphs to chevrons**

In `hero_carousel()`, change the two `.car-nav` button glyphs from arrows to single-angle chevrons (Task 2 makes them flat). Replace:
```python
      <button class="car-nav car-prev" aria-label="Previous">&larr;</button>
      <div class="car-track">
{slides}      </div>
      <button class="car-nav car-next" aria-label="Next">&rarr;</button>
```
with:
```python
      <button class="car-nav car-prev" aria-label="Previous">&lsaquo;</button>
      <div class="car-track">
{slides}      </div>
      <button class="car-nav car-next" aria-label="Next">&rsaquo;</button>
```
(Leave `demos_carousel()`'s `&larr;`/`&rarr;` untouched — its arrows are out of scope.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Add numerals + desc class to the Why-TikZ reasons**

Replace the `.wts-strip` block inside `why_tikz_band()` (keep the surrounding `r"""..."""` raw string and the existing copy verbatim):
```python
    <div class="wts-strip">
      <div class="wts-item"><b>Vector quality</b><span>Crisp at any zoom, sharp in print &mdash; no pixelated screenshots.</span></div>
      <div class="wts-item"><b>Native to your paper</b><span>Same fonts, math, and <code>\ref</code>/<code>\cite</code> as the document.</span></div>
      <div class="wts-item"><b>Text, so it's diffable</b><span>It's source &mdash; version it in git, tweak one number, recompile.</span></div>
    </div>
```
with:
```python
    <div class="wts-strip">
      <div class="wts-item"><span class="wts-num">01</span><b>Vector quality</b><span class="wts-desc">Crisp at any zoom, sharp in print &mdash; no pixelated screenshots.</span></div>
      <div class="wts-item"><span class="wts-num">02</span><b>Native to your paper</b><span class="wts-desc">Same fonts, math, and <code>\ref</code>/<code>\cite</code> as the document.</span></div>
      <div class="wts-item"><span class="wts-num">03</span><b>Text, so it's diffable</b><span class="wts-desc">It's source &mdash; version it in git, tweak one number, recompile.</span></div>
    </div>
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Delete the "Why not ChatGPT" subtitle**

In `why_opentikz_band()`, delete this line entirely:
```python
    <p class="why-ot-sub">You can &mdash; here's what changes when the edit is anchored to a real template.</p>
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Add the `getstarted_band()` helper**

Insert a new function immediately **before** `def home_page(` (after `why_opentikz_band()`):
```python
def getstarted_band() -> str:
    """On-page install path: the two real Claude Code plugin commands (verbatim
    from the How-to-use page's recommended card), with a link out to the README
    for the other install methods."""
    return f"""
  <section class="getstarted" id="get-started">
    <h2>Get started in two lines</h2>
    <p class="gs-lede">Add OpenTikZ to Claude Code, then just describe the figure you want.</p>
    <div class="gs-cmds">
      <pre><code>/plugin marketplace add {REPO_URL}</code></pre>
      <pre><code>/plugin install opentikz@opentikz</code></pre>
    </div>
    <p class="gs-note">Send them as two separate Claude Code messages, then use it with
      <code>/opentikz:using-opentikz</code>. <a href="{REPO_URL}#readme">More ways to install &rarr;</a></p>
  </section>
"""
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Rewire `home_page()` — compute the new band and reorder**

In `home_page()`, just after `why_opentikz = why_opentikz_band()`, add:
```python
    getstarted = getstarted_band()
```
Then in the returned markup, change the hero CTA row from:
```python
{hero_carousel(showcase_items)}    <div class="cta-row">
      <a class="btn btn-primary" href="skills/">Get started</a>
      <a class="btn btn-ghost" href="browse/">Browse the library &rarr;</a>
    </div>
  </section>
{howto_section}
{why_opentikz}
{why_tikz}
  <section class="roadmap">
    <h2>On the roadmap</h2>
    <div class="roadmap-cards">
      <article class="rm-card">
        <span class="rm-tag">in development · next release</span>
        <h3>Prompt-to-diagram <span>natural language → TikZ</span></h3>
        <p>Describe the figure you want; get editable TikZ you can drop straight into
           the library — assembled from templates and skills.</p>
      </article>
      <article class="rm-card">
        <span class="rm-tag">in development · next release</span>
        <h3>Graph-to-diagram <span>graph / spec → TikZ</span></h3>
        <p>Give a node–edge spec (JSON · DOT · adjacency); get a laid-out, editable
           figure you can refine like any template.</p>
      </article>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="cta-band">
```
to:
```python
{hero_carousel(showcase_items)}    <div class="cta-row">
      <a class="btn btn-primary" href="browse/">Browse the library &rarr;</a>
    </div>
  </section>
{getstarted}
{howto_section}
{why_tikz}
{why_opentikz}
  <section class="cta-band">
```
(The hero `Get started` button is deleted; `Browse the library` is now the single primary CTA. The entire `<section class="roadmap">…</section>` block is gone. `{why_tikz}` now precedes `{why_opentikz}`.)

- [ ] **Step 7: Repoint the dead `#roadmap` link on the How-to-use page**

In `skills_page()`, scenario-3 card, change:
```python
        <p>Describe a figure with no starting template, or hand over a node–edge spec.
           Prompt-to-diagram and graph-to-diagram are
           <a href="../index.html#roadmap">on the roadmap</a>.</p>
```
to (drop the link — the target no longer exists):

```python
        <p>Describe a figure with no starting template, or hand over a node–edge spec.
```

- [ ] **Step 8: Build and verify markup**

Run:
```bash
cd /Users/xyz/Documents/Projects/opentikz && python3 tools/build_site.py && \
echo "--- present (expect 1,1,3,1,1) ---"; \
grep -c 'id="get-started"' site/index.html; \
grep -c '/plugin install opentikz@opentikz' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'wts-num' site/index.html; \

echo "--- absent (expect 0,0,0,0) ---"; \
grep -c 'On the roadmap' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'why-ot-sub' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'href="skills/">Get started' site/index.html; \
grep -c 'index.html#roadmap' site/skills/index.html; \
echo "--- order: why-tikz line < why-ot line ---"; \
grep -n 'class="why-tikz-slim"\|class="why-ot"' site/index.html
```
Expected: build prints `built site/ — …`; present group = `1 1 3 1 1`; absent group = `0 0 0 0`; the order grep lists `why-tikz-slim` on a **smaller** line number than `why-ot`.

### Task 2: CSS — Get-started, numbered Why-TikZ cards, centered Why-not, flat hero chevrons, rule cleanup

**Files:**
- Modify: `tools/build_site.py` — the `STYLE_CSS` raw string: `.why-tikz-slim` + `.wts-*` (~L1105-1110), `.why-ot` (~L1113), `.car-nav` area (add a `.hero-carousel .car-nav` override near L1166), the roadmap CSS block (~L1226-1237, delete). Add a new `.getstarted` block.

**Interfaces:**
- Consumes Task 1's HTML hooks: `.getstarted`, `.gs-lede`, `.gs-cmds`, `.gs-note`, `.wts-num`, `.wts-desc`, `.hero-carousel .car-nav` glyphs.

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the Get-started section styles**

In `STYLE_CSS`, immediately after the `.cta-band{padding:42px 0; border-top:1px solid var(--line)}` line (~L1102), add:
```css
/* ---------- get started (on-page install) ---------- */
.getstarted{padding:42px 0; border-top:1px solid var(--line); text-align:center}
.getstarted h2{font-family:"Fraunces",serif; font-weight:600; font-size:1.7rem; margin:0 0 .25em; letter-spacing:-.01em}
.gs-lede{color:var(--muted); margin:0 0 18px; font-size:1rem}
.gs-cmds{display:grid; gap:10px; max-width:560px; margin:0 auto}
.gs-cmds pre{margin:0}
.gs-cmds code{display:block; text-align:left; padding:10px 14px; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:6px; background:var(--paper); color:var(--ink); font:.9rem "IBM Plex Mono",monospace}
.gs-note{color:var(--muted); margin:16px auto 0; max-width:54ch; font-size:.9rem; line-height:1.5}
.gs-note a{color:var(--otblue); text-decoration:none; border-bottom:1px solid var(--otblue)}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Restyle the Why-TikZ strip into numbered cards + add the section-title h2 + border**

Replace the four current lines:
```css
.why-tikz-slim{max-width:980px; margin:0 auto; padding:40px 28px; text-align:center}
.wts-strip{display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr); gap:26px; margin-top:18px; text-align:left}
.wts-item{display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:4px}
.wts-item b{font:600 .95rem system-ui; color:var(--otblue)}
.wts-item span{font:.85rem/1.45 system-ui; opacity:.72}
```
with:
```css
.why-tikz-slim{max-width:980px; margin:0 auto; padding:42px 28px 42px; border-top:1px solid var(--line); text-align:center}
.why-tikz-slim h2{font-family:"Fraunces",serif; font-weight:600; font-size:1.7rem; margin:0 0 .25em; letter-spacing:-.01em}
.wts-strip{display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr); gap:18px; margin-top:22px; text-align:left}
.wts-item{display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:7px; padding:18px 18px 20px; border:1px solid var(--line-strong); border-radius:10px; background:var(--paper)}
.wts-num{font:600 .8rem "IBM Plex Mono",monospace; color:var(--otorange); letter-spacing:.04em}
.wts-item b{font:600 1rem "Fraunces",serif; color:var(--ink)}
.wts-desc{font:.9rem/1.5 "IBM Plex Sans",sans-serif; color:var(--muted)}
```
(The `padding:28px` is on the section already; the section `max-width:980px` value reads `padding:40px 28px` originally — keep horizontal `28px`. The `@media(max-width:680px){.wts-strip{grid-template-columns:1fr}}` line right below stays unchanged.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Center the "Why not ChatGPT" section, keep card text left**

Change:
```css
.why-ot{padding:42px 0; border-top:1px solid var(--line)}
```
to:
```css
.why-ot{padding:42px 0; border-top:1px solid var(--line); text-align:center}
```
Then, so the comparison cards' bullet text doesn't inherit centering, change the `.cmp-cards` line:
```css
.cmp-cards{display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:18px}
```
to:
```css
.cmp-cards{display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:18px; text-align:left}
```

- [ ] **Step 4: Make the hero carousel arrows flat chevrons**

Immediately after the existing `.car-nav:hover{border-color:var(--otblue); color:var(--otblue)}` line (~L1168), add a hero-scoped override (leaves the demos carousel's circular arrows untouched):
```css
.hero-carousel .car-nav{width:34px; height:34px; padding:0; border:none; background:none;
  font-size:1.9rem; line-height:1; color:var(--muted); box-shadow:none}
.hero-carousel .car-nav:hover{color:var(--otblue); border:none; transform:none}
```

- [ ] **Step 5: Delete the now-unused roadmap CSS**

Remove the contiguous roadmap block from `STYLE_CSS` — the `/* roadmap teaser */` comment (if present) plus every rule whose selector starts `.roadmap`, `.roadmap-cards`, `.rm-card`, `.rm-tag`, `.rm-card h3`, `.rm-card h3 span`, `.rm-card p` (the run beginning `.roadmap{padding:42px 0; …}` through `.rm-card p{…font-size:.93rem}`). Read the file around that range first to delete the exact span.

- [ ] **Step 6: Build and verify CSS**

Run:
```bash
cd /Users/xyz/Documents/Projects/opentikz && python3 tools/build_site.py && \
echo "--- present (expect >=1 each) ---"; \
grep -c '\.getstarted{' site/assets/style.css; \
grep -c '\.wts-num{' site/assets/style.css; \
grep -c '\.hero-carousel \.car-nav{' site/assets/style.css; \
grep -c '\.why-tikz-slim h2{' site/assets/style.css; \
grep -c 'text-align:center}' site/assets/style.css; \
echo "--- why-ot centered (expect 1) ---"; \
grep -c '\.why-ot{[^}]*text-align:center' site/assets/style.css; \
echo "--- roadmap CSS gone (expect 0,0) ---"; \
grep -c '\.rm-card' site/assets/style.css; \
grep -c '\.roadmap{' site/assets/style.css
```
Expected: present group each `>=1`; why-ot centered = `1`; roadmap group = `0 0`. Build prints `built site/ — …`.

- [ ] **Step 7: Final visual sanity — serve and eyeball (optional but recommended)**

Run:
```bash
cd /Users/xyz/Documents/Projects/opentikz/site && python3 -m http.server 8137 >/dev/null 2>&1 &
echo "open http://localhost:8137/ — verify: hero has only Browse button + flat chevrons; Get-started section below hero with two command boxes; 4-ways; Why TikZ (3 numbered cards); Why not ChatGPT (centered title, no subtitle); no roadmap; single rule between every section. Then: kill %1"
```
Expected: page matches the spec's section order and styling; no console 404 for `#roadmap`.

- [ ] **Step 8: Commit**

```bash
cd /Users/xyz/Documents/Projects/opentikz && git add tools/build_site.py && \
git commit -m "Home CSS: get-started band, numbered Why-TikZ cards, centered Why-not, flat hero chevrons, drop roadmap CSS
```

## Self-Review

**1. Spec coverage:**
- Reorder Why TikZ above Why-not → Task 1 Step 6. ✅
- Why TikZ h2 to shared 1.7rem treatment → Task 2 Step 2. ✅
- Why-not title centered + subtitle deleted → Task 1 Step 4 (subtitle), Task 2 Step 3 (center). ✅
- Horizontal rule between every section → `.getstarted` (T2S1), `.why-tikz-slim` border-top added (T2S2); `.howto`, `.why-ot`, `.cta-band` already have one; `.showcase` first has none. ✅
- Delete roadmap → Task 1 Step 6 (markup) + Task 2 Step 5 (CSS) + Task 1 Step 7 (dead-link fix). ✅
- Why-TikZ reasons as bordered numbered cards, site fonts → Task 1 Step 3 (numerals/markup) + Task 2 Step 2 (cards). ✅
- Hero Get-started button deleted, Browse primary → Task 1 Step 6. ✅
- Hero carousel flat chevrons → Task 1 Step 2 (glyphs) + Task 2 Step 4 (flat CSS). ✅
- New on-page Get-started section, real commands, README link, `#get-started` anchor → Task 1 Step 5. ✅

**2. Placeholder scan:** No TBD/TODO; every code step shows the exact before/after. ✅

**3. Type consistency:** `getstarted_band()` defined (T1S5) and called via `getstarted = getstarted_band()` then `{getstarted}` (T1S6). CSS hooks emitted in Task 1 (`.gs-*`, `.wts-num`, `.wts-desc`, `.hero-carousel .car-nav`) all have matching rules in Task 2. Verification greps reference the same class names. ✅
